name: Glutenberg Blonde
note: Dedicated gluten-free brewery in Montreal, founded 2010 by Julien Niquet and David Cayer; first beer launched 2011. Naturally gluten-free from unmalted grains.

name: Glutenberg American Pale Ale
note: Brewery's first hoppy beer; citrus/apricot notes.

name: Glutenberg IPA
note: Naturally gluten-free.

name: Glutenberg Red (Rousse)
note: Brewed with two types of roasted chestnuts; caramel and coffee notes.

name: Glutenberg White
note: Grainy profile from amaranth and quinoa.

name: Glutenberg Stout
note: Opaque black; cocoa and roasted cereal aromas.

name: Glutenberg Gose
note: Seasonal release.

name: Glutenberg Double IPA
note: 99 IBU.

name: Glutenberg Non-Alcoholic Blonde
note: Gluten-free non-alcoholic beer.

name: Grapefruit IPA
note: 100% dedicated GF brewery in Seattle; FAQ states 'every single ingredient in every single one of our beers is naturally gluten-free... primarily made from malted millet.' Malt sourced from Grouse Malting Co.

name: Vanishing Point Pale Ale
note: Flagship; dedicated GF facility.

name: Meteor Shower Pale Lager
note: Approx 125 calories; flagship.

name: Shrouded Summit Belgian White Ale
note: Flagship.

name: Watchstander Stout
note: Flagship stout.

name: Peak Buster Double IPA
note: Hopped with Eureka, Centennial and Summit.

name: It Came From The Haze Hazy IPA
note: Flagship hazy IPA.

name: Kick Step IPA
note: Flagship IPA.

name: Fat Randy's IPA
note: Dedicated GF brewery in Golden, Colorado; grain sourced near Grouse Malt House. Some sources list 65 IBU.

name: Favorite Blonde
note: Year-round flagship.

name: Riva Stout
note: Coffee/dark chocolate/caramel; silky dry finish.

name: Buckwit Belgian
note: Wit style with coriander and orange peel.

name: Big Henry Hazy IPA
note: Juicy citrus hops including Mosaic.

name: Boombastic Hazy IPA
note: Rotating/seasonal hazy IPA.

name: IPA No. 5
note: Dedicated GF brewery in Portland OR; Belgian-style candi sugar; Crystal and Santiam hops.

name: Pale Ale (Inclusion / Dry-Hopped Pale Ale)
note: Original beer since 2011; multiple GABF medals. Dry-hopped Cascade/Meridian.

name: Dark Ale
note: Also brewed with Eckert malted rice in a variant; GABF medal-winner. Some listings show 4.5% for the malted-rice 'Dark Mild' version.

name: 99 Light
note: 92 calories, 1g sugar, 6g carbs per 12oz.

name: Olallie
note: Rotating seasonal fruit, rosehips, Crystal hops.

name: Cascadian Dark Ale (Psychrophilic)
note: Ephemeral Series limited release.

name: Lawless Village IPA
note: Bellfield's About page: uses low-gluten barley malt plus maize/buckwheat and adds Clarex enzyme; routinely tests below 10ppm. Certified by Coeliac UK. Founded 2015 by two coeliacs. Classified gluten-removed as it uses barley.

name: Bohemian Pilsner
note: Saaz hops; certified by Coeliac UK. Uses barley malt with enzyme.

name: Craft Lager
note: Silver 'Best Lager' Scottish Beer Awards 2023. Uses barley malt with enzyme.

name: Jex-Blake Mosaic IPA
note: Double dry-hopped Mosaic IPA.

name: Auld Reekie Black
note: Full-bodied porter with chocolate, coffee and berry notes.

name: Fire Island IPA
note: Session IPA with citrus and malty character.

name: Green's Amber Ale
note: Brewed at De Proefbrouwerij, Lochristi, Belgium since 2004. Naturally GF.

name: Green's IPA
note: Simcoe, Amarillo, Nelson hops.

name: Green's Dubbel Ale
note: Described as the world's first dubbel made with no barley.

name: Green's Tripel Ale
note: Naturally GF.

name: Green's Dry-Hopped Lager
note: International pale lager; some listings 4.7-5.0%.

name: O'Brien Pale Ale
note: First gluten-free beer produced in Australia (2005). Brewed at Rebellion Brewery near Ballarat. Naturally GF; endorsed by Coeliac Australia.

name: O'Brien Premium Lager
note: Winner at first international GF beer awards UK 2006.

name: O'Brien Brown Ale
note: AIBA Gold Medal; World Beer Cup silver 2010.

name: O'Brien Natural Light
note: Described as world's first naturally brewed GF low-alcohol beer.

name: O'Brien Belgian Ale
note: AIBA Gold Medal-winning seasonal.

name: O'Brien Stout
note: AIBA Gold Medal-winning seasonal with loyal following.

name: Daura Damm
note: Launched 2006; classified 'crafted to remove gluten.' Brewed from same barley malt as Estrella Damm; gluten broken down via hydrolyzation and filtration, certified by CSIC using R5 ELISA. EU label states below 3ppm; US retail lists <6 ppm.

name: Daura Damm Marzen
note: Stronger amber gluten-removed variant in the Daura range.

name: Estrella Galicia Gluten Free
note: Gluten-removed via enzyme; certified by FACE (Spanish Coeliac Federation), crossed-grain symbol. ABV listed 5.5% in Spain, 4.7% on some export listings.

name: Omission Lager
note: Crafted to remove gluten; adds Brewers Clarex during fermentation, tested by R5 Competitive ELISA. Brewed by Widmer Brothers, Portland OR; launched 2012.

name: Omission Pale Ale
note: Cascade hop-forward; crafted to remove gluten.

name: Omission IPA
note: NW-style IPA with Cascade and Summit hops; crafted to remove gluten.

name: Omission Ultimate Light
note: Low-calorie light beer; crafted to remove gluten.

name: New Planet Blonde Ale
note: Reformulated to remove sorghum; naturally GF. Brewed in Colorado.

name: New Planet Pale Ale (Off Grid)
note: Sterling and Cascade hops; naturally GF.

name: New Planet 3R Raspberry Ale
note: Naturally GF raspberry ale.

name: New Planet Tread Lightly Ale
note: Labeled 'crafted to remove gluten'; gluten-reduced session ale.

name: New Planet Seclusion IPA
note: Crafted to remove gluten (gluten-reduced).

name: New Grist
note: First GF beer granted US government label approval (Milwaukee, WI). Every batch tested for gluten. Naturally GF.

name: New Grist Ginger Style Ale
note: Ginger variant of New Grist.

name: Stone Delicious IPA
note: Released Jan 2015 (Escondido, CA); crafted with Clarex enzyme to break gluten protein chains. Lemondrop and El Dorado hops. Gluten-reduced.

name: Pyro American Pale Ale
note: Dedicated GF brewery in St. Paul, Minnesota. Naturally GF flagship.

name: Most Coast IPA
note: Flagship IPA.

name: Midway American Lager
note: Naturally GF lager.

name: Auntie M's Irish Red Ale
note: Warrior hops.

name: Black Pepper Porter
note: Brewed with cracked black peppercorns.

name: Roasted Coffee Strong Ale
note: Coffee ale; naturally GF.

name: Tart Cherry Sour
note: Fruit sour brewed on sorghum and syrup.

name: Aurochs Blonde Ale
note: Dedicated GF brewery in Pittsburgh PA. Brewery ceased operations end of 2024.

name: Aurochs Session IPA
note: Citrus/pine/grapefruit. Brewery closed end of 2024.

name: Aurochs Amber Ale
note: Cascade hops. Brewery closed end of 2024.

name: Aurochs Porter
note: Saaz and Simcoe hops. Brewery closed end of 2024.

name: Aurochs Light Lager
note: 85 calories; Hallertau/Tettnang/Saaz hops. Brewery closed end of 2024.

name: Aurochs Hefe (Hefequinoa)
note: GF Hefeweizen with banana and clove. Brewery closed end of 2024.

name: Aurochs Hazy IPA
note: Mango/orange. Brewery closed end of 2024.

name: Aurochs Pumpkin Ale
note: 2020 GABF Gold Medal (GF category); brewed with pumpkin, molasses, honey, spices. Brewery closed end of 2024.

name: Brunehaut Bio Blonde
note: Organic (Certisys BE-BIO-01); brewed with own barley and a natural gluten-removal process to under 5ppm. 2017 World Beer Awards gold 'World's Best Gluten-free Beer.'

name: Brunehaut Bio Ambree
note: Organic, gluten-removed. Caramel and peppery notes.

name: Brunehaut Bio Triple
note: Organic, gluten-removed; citrus and apricot.

name: Brunehaut Bio Blanche
note: Organic white beer, gluten-removed.

name: Brunehaut Bio Saison
note: Organic saison, gluten-removed.

name: Two Bays Pale Ale
note: Dedicated GF brewery on the Mornington Peninsula, Victoria; Citra and Loral hops. Endorsed by Coeliac Australia.

name: Two Bays Pacific Ale
note: Naturally GF.

name: Two Bays XPA
note: Naturally GF.

name: Two Bays IPA
note: Naturally GF.

name: Wilde Pale Ale
note: Mid-strength; FODMAP friendly; endorsed by Coeliac Australia. Naturally GF.

name: Hahn Ultra Crisp
note: Uses rice instead of wheat/barley; low-carb GF lager from mainstream brewer Lion.

name: Rustic Badger Farmhouse Ale
note: Dedicated GF brewery in Madison, WI; Belgian yeast strain. Naturally GF.

name: Event Horizon Blonde
note: Dedicated GF (Southern California); 120 calories per can. Naturally GF.

name: Divine Millsner Lager
note: Pilsner-style; naturally GF.

name: Particle Haze IPA
note: Pineapple and citrus, mild bitterness.

name: Moka Diosa Stout
note: Iced-mocha inspired stout; naturally GF.

name: Third Contact IPA
note: Naturally GF IPA.

name: Dogfish Head Tweason'ale
note: Naturally GF, brewed with sorghum, strawberries and buckwheat honey; seasonal, now discontinued.

name: Redbridge
note: Launched Dec 2006, first nationally available US sorghum beer. Naturally GF. Reportedly discontinued/phasing out; some retailers list ABV up to 4.8%.

name: Coors Peak Copper Lager
note: Certified GF by Gluten Intolerance Group; no sorghum, no enzyme; launched 2015. Discontinued.

name: Coors Peak Golden Lager
note: Added March 2016; PNW hops. Discontinued.

name: Bard's (Original Sorghum Malt Beer)
note: Described as North America's first GF beer; only GF beer to malt its sorghum. Hersbrucker and Tettnang hops. Naturally GF.

name: Peroni Nastro Azzurro Gluten Free
note: Same ingredients as regular Peroni including barley; enzyme added at end of brewing removes gluten. Endorsed by Italian Coeliac Association (AIC).

name: Peter, Pale and Mary Gluten Free
note: Contract-brewed at De Proef, Belgium; GF version of Mikkeller's APA, lab-tested below 20ppm. Centennial and Amarillo hops.

name: Vagabond Pale Ale
note: BrewDog's first GF beer; 100% malted barley plus enzyme to remove gluten. Centennial and Amarillo hops.

name: Glütiny Pale Ale
note: Launched Jan 2016; 'crafted to remove gluten,' comes in under 10ppm. Equinox, Chinook, Cascade, Crystal hops. Largely discontinued/legacy.

name: Glütiny Golden Ale
note: Crafted to remove gluten, under 10ppm; Nugget, Goldings, Cascade hops. Largely discontinued/legacy.

name: Forager Gluten-Free Lager
note: Described as BC's first craft GF lager; Brave and Sterling hops. Naturally GF, vegan.

name: Forager Gluten-Free Pale Ale
note: Brave, Sterling, Cascade, Centennial hops. Naturally GF.

name: Nickel Brook Gluten Free
note: Described as first GF beer made in Ontario (since 2011); brewed with sorghum, demerara sugar and pear juice. Naturally GF; verify current availability.

name: La Blonde Gluten Free
note: Enzyme process fragments gluten to under 20mg/L; certified by AFDIAG (French coeliac assoc.), crossed-grain logo. Strisselspalt hops.

name: La Rousse Gluten Free
note: World's Best Amber (European Beer Stars 2018); gluten-removed. Strisselspalt hops.

name: La Cristal IPA Gluten Free
note: Gluten-removed IPA; certified by AFDIAG.

name: La Bleue Gluten Free
note: Brewed with blueberry juice; gluten-removed.

name: Hambleton GFA (Gluten Free Ale)
note: One of the UK's earliest GF beers (2005); enzymatic process to under 20ppm. Cascade, Liberty, Challenger hops. Exported to US as 'Toleration.'

name: Hambleton GFL (Gluten Free Lager)
note: Premium lager; gluten hydrolyzed to under 20ppm; wheat-free.

name: Toleration
note: US export brand name for Hambleton GFA; gluten-removed.

name: POD Vanilla Oatmeal Stout
note: Salford, Manchester; whole range gluten-free & vegan since ~2015-16, gluten-reducing enzyme + double testing to under 10ppm. Madagascan vanilla.

name: AVA Hoppy Blonde Ale
note: Gluten-removed to under 10ppm; vegan.

name: SYL Dark IPA
note: Gluten-removed; vegan.

name: YES Alcohol-Free American IPA
note: Contains barley, wheat, oats and rye; gluten reduced to under 10ppm. Summit and Amarillo hops.

name: Heathen Pale Ale
note: Sheffield brewery using Brewers Clarex/proline-specific enzyme to under 20ppm, UKAS-lab + lateral-flow tested. Mosaic hops. Since Oct 2024 brewing all beers to GF recipe.

name: Autumn Alt Brew No.03 Stout
note: County Durham brewery; naturally GF from malted rice/millet/quinoa (no barley). Verify current availability.

name: Autumn Alt Brew No.01 Pilsner
note: Bavarian-style; Tettnang hops. Naturally GF.

name: Autumn Alt Brew No.02 Pale Ale
note: Cascade plus English hops. Naturally GF.

name: Steadfast Sorghum Pale Ale
note: Naturally GF, sorghum-based with certified GF oat groats. ABV listed 5.5-6.8% across sources.

name: Steadfast Oatmeal Cream Stout
note: Most popular; hand-roasted certified GF oat groats. Naturally GF.

name: Steadfast Golden Ale
note: Honey, sweet orange peel, coriander, Belgian yeast. Naturally GF.

name: Buck Wild Pale Ale
note: California's first 100% dedicated GF brewery (Oakland). Naturally GF.

name: Buck Wild West Coast IPA
note: Citra/Mosaic/Meridian hops. Naturally GF (contains oats).

name: Buck Wild Amber Ale
note: Naturally GF.

name: Buck Wild Alpenglow Hazy IPA
note: Enigma/Citra/Cashmere hops. Naturally GF (contains oats).

name: Jump Ship Yardarm Lager
note: Marketed gluten-free (<20ppm) but brewed from malted barley; classified gluten-removed. World's Best Non-Alcoholic Lager, World Beer Awards 2021. Citra & Styrian Bobek hops.

name: Jump Ship Shore Leave Pale Ale
note: Amarillo & El Dorado hops; marketed GF but barley-based (gluten-removed).

name: Estrella Damm Daura IPA
note: IPA variant in the gluten-removed Daura range; barley malt with proprietary gluten-reduction process.

name: Celia Saison
note: Belgian-inspired saison originally brewed by The Alchemist; naturally GF, brewed from sorghum.
